stem: A factory produces microchips using two sources. Fabrication Line A handles 80% of production with a known defect rate of 3.2%. Fabrication Line B handles 20% of production, but its defect rate is uncertain — recent quality audits suggest it lies between 5.7% and 9.0%, depending on raw material batch quality. A randomly selected microchip is found to be defective. What is the probability it came from Fabrication Line B?

ground_truth.answer: P(Fabrication Line B | defective) ranges from 0.308 to 0.413 as Fabrication Line B's defect rate varies from 5.7% to 9.0%. Point estimate at 7.3%: 0.363.

solution_steps: Apply Bayes' theorem: P(B|def) = P(def|B)×P(B) / [P(def|A)×P(A) + P(def|B)×P(B)].
The interval [0.308, 0.413] captures the range of posterior probabilities.
common_failure_modes: Using only the midpoint defect rate and providing a single answer
Comparing defect rates directly without applying Bayes' theorem
